title: साईं बाबा शेज आरती
devanagariText:
॥ श्री साईं बाबा शेज आरती ॥
॥ रात्रिकालीन शयन आरती ॥

ओवाळू आरती माझ्या सद्‍गुरुनाथा, माझ्या साईनाथा।
पांचाही तत्त्वांचा दीप लावूनी उजळले,
आत्मज्ञानें ज्योत, पाजळत राहे उज्ज्वला।
लावीला दीप, घृतसंपन्नाचा तो॥

उजळले माझे हृदय, तव कृपेचा प्रकाश।
सद्‌गुरू साईनाथ, ओवाळू आरती तुझी दीनदयाळा॥ १॥

भक्ताँच्या हृदयीं, बसवलें तव रूप।
साई माउली, कृपेच्या सागरा, आम्ही शरण तुला।
राखी शरणागत, दीन दुबळ्याँना,
तूच आमचा माता, तूच आमचा पिता॥

तूच आमचा गुरु, तूच आमचा देव।
साई साई, शयन करी, आम्हाँ कृपादृष्टी पाही॥ २॥

श्रीसच्चिदानन्द सद्‍गुरू साईनाथ महाराज की जय।
रात्रीं शेजारती करूनी, बाबाँना शयन करवितो।
साईनाथ, तव शयन सुखद होवो,
भक्तजन आम्ही, सदा तुझ्या चरणीं॥

पहाट होईल, पुन्हा जागवू तुला।
तोवरी सुखशयन, करी माउली गुरू साईनाथा।
तूच आमचा त्राता, तूच आमचा दाता,
सदा तुझ्या चरणीं, राहो माझा चित्ता॥ ३॥

॥ शयनमन्त्र ॥

ॐ राजाधिराज योगिराज परब्रह्म,
श्री सच्चिदानन्द सद्‍गुरू साईनाथ महाराज की जय!

॥ इति श्री साईं बाबा शेज आरती सम्पूर्ण ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Sai Baba Shej Aarti ||
|| Ratrikalin Shayana Aarti ||

Ovalu Aarti Majhya Sadgurunatha, Majhya Sainatha.
Panchahi Tattvanchya Dipa Lavuni Ujale,
Atmajnane Jyota, Pajalat Rahe Ujjvala.
Lavila Dipa, Ghritasampannacha To.

Ujale Majhe Hridaya, Tava Kripecha Prakasha.
Sadguru Sainatha, Ovalu Aarti Tujhi Dinadayala. ||1||

Bhaktanchya Hridayi, Basavale Tava Rupa.
Sai Mauli, Kripechya Sagara, Amhi Sharana Tula.
Rakhi Sharanagata, Dina Dublyanna,
Tuch Amacha Mata, Tuch Amacha Pita.

Tuch Amacha Guru, Tuch Amacha Deva.
Sai Sai, Shayana Kari, Amhan Kripadrishti Pahi. ||2||

Shri Sachchidananda Sadguru Sainatha Maharaja Ki Jaya.
Ratri Shejaarti Karuni, Babanna Shayana Karavito.
Sainatha, Tava Shayana Sukhada Hovo,
Bhaktajana Amhi, Sada Tujhya Charani.

Pahata Hoila, Punha Jagavu Tula.
Tovari Sukhashayana, Kari Mauli Guru Sainatha.
Tuch Amacha Trata, Tuch Amacha Data,
Sada Tujhya Charani, Raho Majha Chitta. ||3||

|| Shayana Mantra ||

Om Rajadhiraja Yogiraja Parabrahma,
Shri Sachchidananda Sadguru Sainatha Maharaja Ki Jaya!

|| Iti Shri Sai Baba Shej Aarti Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning: मेरे सद्गुरुनाथ साईनाथ की आरती। पाँचों तत्त्वों का दीप जलाकर आत्मज्ञान की ज्योत उज्ज्वल की। आपकी कृपा का प्रकाश से हृदय उजला हुआ। भक्तों के हृदय में आपका रूप बसाया है। कृपा के सागर, हम शरण हैं — शरणागतों, दीन-दुर्बलों की रक्षा करें। आप ही माता, पिता, गुरु और देव हैं। सच्चिदानन्द सद्गुरु साईनाथ महाराज की जय! रात्रि की शेज-आरती करके बाबा को शयन कराते हैं। प्रभात होगी तो पुनः जगाएँगे, तब तक सुख-शयन करें गुरु साईनाथ। आप ही त्राता हैं, दाता हैं, सदा चरणों में चित्त रहे।
benefits: Night-time (Shej) aarti for Sai Baba — the closing aarti of the day at Shirdi
Traditionally invoked to establish a peaceful atmosphere for restful sleep under sai baba's protection
Surrendering to the Guru before sleep dissolves the day's anxieties and worries
Strengthens the Guru-disciple bond and deepens spiritual surrender
Invokes Sai as Sachidananda Parabrahma — the supreme truth, consciousness, bliss
Regular recitation brings divine protection through the night
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Shree Sai Baba of Shirdi, Sai Baba Shej Aarti (साईं बाबा शेज आरती) stands as a traditional worship song (aarti). This sacred composition is sourced from Shirdi Sai Baba Sansthan Tradition and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to regular recitation brings divine protection through the night, night-time (Shej) aarti for Sai Baba — the closing aarti of the day at Shirdi, and strengthen the Guru-disciple bond and deepen spiritual surrender. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Guru Purnima. At 3 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 5 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Sai Baba Shej Aarti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Sai Baba Shej Aarti (night aarti) in Marathi with Hindi meaning and transliteration. Shirdi night-time shayana aarti.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Sai Baba Shej Aarti?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sai Baba Shej Aarti (शेज आरती — Bed-aarti / Night-closure aarti) is the fourth and final daily aarti performed at the Samadhi Mandir of Shirdi Sai Baba — the great Sufi-Hindu saint of Shirdi (1838-1918) — and at Sai temples worldwide. It is the night-aarti performed before Baba retires to His shej (sacred bed/cradle) for the night, traditionally at 10:30 PM at Shirdi (varying slightly by season and festival). The aarti is composed predominantly in Marathi with Sanskrit shlokas, opening with Owalu Aarti Majhya Sadgurunatha — Majhya Sainatha (I wave the aarti for my Sadguru-nath, my Sai-nath). The Shej Aarti is the most intimate and tender of the four daily aartis as it accompanies Baba to His resting bed, asking Him to bestow peaceful night-rest, dreams, and protection until the morning Kakad-aarti. The text is preserved in classical Shirdi Sai sampradaya and recited daily. (Sources: Shirdi Sai Sansthan aarti book; Sai Satcharita.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the four daily aartis of Shirdi Sai Baba?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shirdi Sai Sansthan performs four aartis daily at the Samadhi Mandir, marking the four sandhi (transition periods) of the day. (1) Kakad Aarti (काकड आरती — pre-dawn awakening aarti) — performed approximately at 4:30 AM to wake Baba; uses cotton-torch (kakad) lamp; opens with Joduniya Kara Charani Theviya Mathaa. (2) Madhyana Aarti (मध्यान आरती — noon aarti) — performed at 12:00 PM, the most elaborate aarti with naivedya (food offering); opens with Ghee Niranjan Karuni Bali Lavi Aarti Sayicha. (3) Dhup Aarti (धूप आरती — sunset incense aarti) — performed at sunset (around 6:30 PM), accompanied by dhup-incense; opens with Aarati Sayibaba Saukhyadatara Jiva. (4) Shej Aarti (शेज आरती — night sleeping aarti) — performed at 10:30 PM as the day closes; opens with Owalu Aarti Majhya Sadgurunatha. Each aarti has its specific time, mood, naivedya, and verses; together they encompass the full daily worship cycle. (Sources: Shirdi Sai Sansthan paddhati; Sai Satcharita Chapter 4.)

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Sai Baba Shej Aarti and other aartis?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shirdi Sai Baba aartis are predominantly composed in Marathi by several devotees and saints during Baba's lifetime and immediately after His Mahasamadhi (1918): (1) Krishnarao Jageshwar Bhishma — primary compiler of the Sai aarti book Sri Sai Baba Sansthan Aarti and composer of several aarti verses. (2) Madhav Adkar — composer of various Shirdi devotional verses. (3) Das Ganu Maharaj (Ganesh Dattatreya Sahasrabuddhe) — Baba's contemporary and a prolific composer who wrote much of the aarti material. (4) Upasani Maharaj — Baba's beloved disciple. (5) Dada Kelkar and Tatya Patil — early Shirdi devotees. The aartis also incorporate Sanskrit verses from traditional sources — Sri Sai Stavan Manjari, slokas from Bhagavad Gita, and adaptations of Tukaram and Namdeo abhangas. The Shej Aarti specifically uses Owalu Aarti from earlier Marathi devotional tradition adapted for Sai Baba. (Sources: Sai Sansthan Aarti compilation history; Das Ganu biography.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Owalu Aarti Majhya Sadgurunatha?
seoFaqs.answer: Owalu Aarti Majhya Sadgurunatha — Majhya Sainatha is the opening line of the Shej Aarti and translates from Marathi as: I wave (Owalu) the aarti (Aarti) for my (Majhya) Sadguru-nath (Lord-of-true-guru) — my Sai-nath. The succeeding verses describe lighting a lamp (deep) of pancha-tattva (five elements — earth, water, fire, air, ether) ignited by atma-jnana (self-knowledge), illuminating the devotee's heart with Baba's grace, and surrendering the entire being at His feet for the night. The verses specifically ask Baba to: bless the bhakta-hriday (devotee's heart) where Baba's own form (rupa) is enthroned; protect those who have surrendered (sharanagat); grant peaceful sleep filled with Baba's vision; and remain present through the night until morning awakening. The Shej Aarti's tone is uniquely tender and child-like — devotees bidding goodnight to Baba as one would to a beloved father or mother. (Sources: Shirdi Sai aarti book; Sai Satcharita.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting Shej Aarti at night?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shej Aarti is regarded by Sai devotees as the most powerful night-protection prayer with multiple benefits: (1) Peaceful sleep — the aarti calms the mind and removes night-anxieties. (2) Protection from nightmares and disturbing dreams. (3) Removal of nighttime fears especially for children and elderly. (4) Spiritual visions and Baba-darshan in dreams (many devotees report seeing Baba in sleep after consistent Shej Aarti recitation). (5) Family unity — the aarti recited together at night brings the family into a shared devotional moment. (6) Burning of day's accumulated negativity and fatigue. (7) Connection with Shirdi Samadhi Mandir's nightly aarti at 10:30 PM creates a spiritual link with thousands of devotees recitating simultaneously. (8) Surrender of the day's worries to Baba before sleep — a form of yogic samarpan. The aarti is often followed by reading one chapter of Sai Satcharita as the final night-routine. (Sources: Sai bhakta tradition; Shirdi Sai paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: How can devotees perform Shej Aarti at home?
seoFaqs.answer: Home performance of Shej Aarti follows a simple devotional structure suited for any household: (1) Time — between 9:30 PM and 10:30 PM, ideally synchronized with Shirdi's 10:30 PM Shej Aarti time. (2) Place — Sai Baba photo or murti in the puja room or family living area; can be performed in bedroom for personal practice. (3) Items needed — ghee diya (lamp), camphor (kapur), incense (agarbatti), small naivedya (sweet, fruit, dry fruits, sugar candy). (4) Method — light the diya and incense, offer flowers, recite or sing the Shej Aarti, perform ovalan (circular waving of lamp before Baba's image), distribute prasad. (5) Variation — devotees may use the complete Shej Aarti or just key verses (Owalu Aarti, Aata Swami Sukhe Nidra Karaa) for shorter daily practice. (6) Conclude with — reading one chapter of Shri Sai Satcharita (Hemadpant), then Sai bhajan or one round of Sai mantra Om Sri Sai Nathaaya Namah. (7) Special days — Thursdays (Guruvar — Sai's day), Sai Baba Mahasamadhi day (Vijayadashami), Ram Navami (Sai's annual Urus). (Sources: Shirdi Sai Sansthan home-aarti booklet; Sai bhakta-paddhati.)
